title: Optimizing a Memoized React Component for Dependent Arrays Re-renders

statement.markdown: Given a Memoized React component `useMemo` hook with `deps` array containing an object with nested array property references, fix the bug causing it to re-render unnecessarily on state changes. The component uses the `useState` hook to manage its internal state and should only re-render when required. The goal is to optimize the component to update correctly when the dependent arrays change.
statement.inputFormat: A React functional component with a `useState` hook and a `useMemo` hook with a `deps` array containing an object with nested array property references.
statement.outputFormat: A debugged React functional component with the optimized `useMemo` hook and `deps` array.
statement.notes: Ensure the component updates correctly when the dependent arrays change and does not re-render unnecessarily.

examples.explanation: The component should re-render when the `obj.arr` array changes.
The component should re-render when either the `obj.arr` or `obj.nestedArr` arrays change.

hints.text: Use the `JSON.stringify()` method to compare the state of the dependent arrays.

editorial.title: Optimizing Memoized Components with Dependent Arrays
editorial.intuition: When using the `useMemo` hook with `deps` array containing objects with nested array property references, ensure to update the dependent arrays correctly to avoid unnecessary re-renders.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible solution is to use the `JSON.stringify()` method to compare the state of the dependent arrays.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a library like `deep-equal` to compare the state of the dependent arrays.
editorial.optimalSolution: Implement a custom equality function to compare the state of the dependent arrays.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The component updates correctly when the dependent arrays change and does not re-render unnecessarily.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run to demonstrate the correct behavior.
